message:
perf(storage): bind message-insert params directly as SqliteValue and use execute_with_params

Drops one layer of conversion in the message-batch insert hot path.

Previous shape:
    let mut param_values: Vec<ParamValue> = ...;
    param_values.push(ParamValue::from(conversation_id));
    ...
    tx.execute_compat(&sql, &param_values)?;

The frankensqlite `compat` module's `ParamValue` wrapper exists for
the legacy `execute_compat` entry point — it converts each value
through an intermediate enum before reaching the actual SQLite
binding code. The newer `execute_with_params` entry takes
`SqliteValue` directly, skipping the wrapper. For a batch of, say,
100 rows × 8 columns × append cron, the wrapper conversion happens
800 times per batch and is wasted work because every call site
already knows the target SQLite type at compile time.

Changes (all in `src/storage/sqlite.rs`):

- Imports: add `SqliteValue` to the existing `frankensqlite::{...}`
  use list, alongside `Connection`, `Row`, etc.
- `franken_batch_insert_new_messages_with_batch_size`:
  - `Vec<ParamValue>` → `Vec<SqliteValue>`.
  - Eight `ParamValue::from(...)` calls → `SqliteValue::from(...)`
    (conversation_id i64, idx i64, role &str, author Option<&str>,
    created_at Option<i64>, content &str, extra_json_str &str,
    extra_bin Option<&[u8]>).
  - `tx.execute_compat(&sql, &param_values)?` →
    `tx.execute_with_params(&sql, &param_values)?`.
- `franken_batch_insert_new_messages_with_profile_batch_size`
  (the profile-instrumented variant used by the stage-profile
  tests): identical substitution. The `payload_duration`,
  `param_build_duration`, and `execute_duration` instrumentation
  stays intact and now measures the slimmer direct path; the
  `param_build_duration` budget should drop accordingly when
  re-running the profile harness.

The rest of the storage layer continues to use `execute_compat` /
`fparams!` because those call sites issue ad-hoc statements with
heterogeneous bind types; the message-insert hot path is the one
that benefits from the typed-vector route. Behavior is unchanged
— same SQL, same bind order, same conversation_id / idx / role /
author / created_at / content / extra_json / extra_bin column
order — only the binding pathway through frankensqlite differs.
